summary:
The most probable CSF findings are increased white blood cells predominantly as lymphocytes, elevated proteins due to inflammation, alongside decreased glucose concentration suggesting involvement consistent with suspected tuberculous meningitis based on presented symptoms and radiological features.
The likelihood of tuberculous meningitis increases significantly considering all provided signs; thus, expect characteristic alterations like raised intracranial pressure markers including leukocytosis primarily involving lymphocytes, higher protein content secondary to inflammatory response, coupled with hypoglycorrhachia indicating tissue metabolism disruption seen commonly during such infections.
To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects:

Increased lymphocyte counts, high protein level, low glucose suggest tuberculous meningitis fitting presenting picture perfectly. Key indicators include leucopenic pleocytosis, hyperproteinemia, hypo-glucoceuria pointing towards active meningeal tuberculosis infection supported by neuroimaging clues too. To condense further into two core statements without extraneous detail yet retaining essence crucial remains essential task ahead now.
Key Points Condensed Summary: Elevated WBC mainly lympcytic, heightened protein & lowered glucose indicate TB meningitis matching case presentation closely. Neurological manifestations plus imaging support diagnosis strongly implying expected CSF profile noted above accurately reflects underlying pathology observed clinically herein described scenario
